marketState: neutral, bearish near-term tilt inside a mildly bullish seasonal backdrop
summary: Front month ($3.218) sits inside the fair-value band ($2.429-$3.429) but slightly above fair ($2.929), and that premium shrinks naturally over the next three weeks as modeled fair value rises to $3.055 - the market isn't badly mispriced either way. Weather is the key near-term swing factor: 1-7 day demand is running hot (+8.4% vs 5y, rangePos 0.74) but the 8-16 day window has already cooled to -4% vs 5y with demand down 28 units in the last 24h, and daily/weekly technicals confirm a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA cross on both timeframes. Storage adds to the bearish case: a 175 Bcf surplus vs the 5-year average that grew by a bigger-than-forecast 87 Bcf build (13 Bcf bearish surprise). Seasonality offers a countervailing, if weak, bullish tailwind - price is running +2.5pp above the typical year and odds of higher prices rise to 63% at 4 weeks heading into September (the historically strongest month), but reliability is explicitly flagged Low (51pp spread). Managed money is net short (-64.8k contracts, 31st percentile of 5y) which is moderately bearish positioning but not extreme enough to be a strong contrarian signal on its own.

longTerm.entry: $3.00-$3.10 (buy weakness toward fair value rather than chase)
longTerm.riskReward: approx 1:2 blended (1:1.3 to TP1, 1:2.2 to TP2)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% risk, scale in on dips
longTerm.rationale: Seasonality favors higher prices into the strongest month (Sept) with 63% odds higher at 4 weeks and modeled fair value rising from $2.929 to $3.055 by +21D, but the 51pp reliability spread and a 175 Bcf storage surplus (bearish surprise this week) keep conviction low and argue for entering on a pullback toward the lower fair-value band instead of at current levels.
longTerm.invalidation: A storage surprise that keeps beating forecasts bearish, or price breaking decisively below the fair-value band low ($2.43-$2.55), would invalidate the seasonal-strength thesis.

shortTerm.riskReward: approx 1:1.6 blended (1:1 to TP1, 1:1.7 to TP2)
shortTerm.rationale: Daily and weekly technicals both show a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA death cross, the storage build beat forecast by 13 Bcf (bearish surprise), and 8-16 day weather demand has already faded to -4% vs 5y after a sharp 24h drop - the near-term heat that supports the current slight over-fair premium is not confirmed further out, favoring a fade of the current small overvaluation back toward fair.
shortTerm.invalidation: A renewed spike in 1-7D and 8-16D heat demand together with a break above the fair-value band top ($3.43) on strong volume would invalidate the short thesis.

keyDrivers: Storage surplus widened to +175 Bcf vs 5y with a bigger-than-forecast 87 Bcf build (13 Bcf bearish surprise)
Near-term weather hot (+8.4% demand vs 5y) but 8-16 day demand already faded to -4% vs 5y after a 28-unit 24h drop
Daily and weekly technicals both bearish trend with 50<200 EMA death cross; RSI neutral (44-50), no oversold reversal signal yet
Seasonality modestly bullish into September (63% odds higher at 4 weeks) but reliability flagged Low (51pp spread)
Front ($3.218) slightly above fair ($2.929) but within band, and that premium narrows as modeled fair value rises toward $3.055 over 3 weeks
risks: Weather forecasts can reverse quickly (demand already swung -28 units in 24h) which would flip both the short-term and seasonal read
Managed money is net short (31st percentile) - a heat wave or supply disruption could trigger a short-covering squeeze
